surface.name: Text to Speech API via cURL (API key + Free-tier credits)
surface.selection_basis: ElevenLabs API Authentication documents xi-api-key header auth and a first cURL request. The Create speech API reference documents POST https://api.elevenlabs.io/v1/text-to-speech/{voice_id} returning audio. Billing docs assign Free tier automatically at signup with included credit quota. An SDK install is not required.

alternatives_considered.name: ElevenAPI Python/TypeScript Quickstart
alternatives_considered.reason_not_primary: Requires SDK install and speaker playback helpers; the same TTS endpoint works with cURL.

alternatives_considered.name: Speech Synthesis web UI only
alternatives_considered.reason_not_primary: In-browser generate path; not the programmatic API first success.

alternatives_considered.name: cURL GET /v1/models auth probe only
alternatives_considered.reason_not_primary: Proves authentication but does not produce text-to-speech audio.

entry_point.developer_intent: Create an ElevenLabs Free account and API key, then receive synthesized audio over HTTPS without installing an SDK.
assumed_prior_state: No ElevenLabs account, API key, or TTS request is assumed.
A terminal with cURL is available; no language SDK is assumed.

documented_first_success.normalized_outcome: An authenticated cURL POST to https://api.elevenlabs.io/v1/text-to-speech/{voice_id} returns audio bytes (for example written to an mp3 file).
documented_first_success.observable_completion_signal: The HTTP response is successful and the body is playable audio for the requested text.

boundary_evidence.official_label_or_terminal_state: Create speech documents POST /v1/text-to-speech/{voice_id} with xi-api-key and a JSON body containing text and model_id, returning application/octet-stream audio.

documented_first_success.why_this_is_the_boundary: The first meaningful TTS API success is returned speech audio. Authentication docs establish the xi-api-key header pattern with cURL. Free-tier credits are assigned at signup; paid plans and Pay As You Go follow after quota exhaustion.

prerequisites.requirement: An ElevenLabs account (email verification for email signup, or OAuth).
Usable Free-tier credit quota (assigned automatically at signup). Paid subscription or Pay As You Go is required only after Free quota is exhausted.
An API key included as xi-api-key on every request.
A terminal with cURL (or any HTTP client). No language SDK is required.

primary_path.action: Create an ElevenLabs account and verify email if using email signup.
details: Account docs: Get started free, then verify emailed link for email/password signup.
OAuth (Google, Facebook, GitHub) is an alternative signup route.
primary_path.input: Intent to use the ElevenLabs API
primary_path.success_signal: The dashboard and API key settings are reachable.
primary_path.failure_or_wait: Email verification is required before email-signup accounts can use the service.

primary_path.action: Confirm Free-tier credits are available (automatic at signup), or purchase Pay As You Go / upgrade if quota is exhausted.
details: Billing docs: new accounts are automatically assigned to the free tier with included credit quota.
API requests deduct from the quota.
primary_path.success_signal: Subscription shows Free tier with remaining credits.
primary_path.failure_or_wait: Exhausted credits require Pay As You Go or a paid plan before further generation.

primary_path.action: Create an API key in the dashboard and store it securely.
details: Authentication docs: every request must include xi-api-key.
primary_path.success_signal: The key value is available locally.
primary_path.failure_or_wait: Keys may be scope-restricted; Text to Speech permission must be allowed for this route.

primary_path.action: Export the key as ELEVENLABS_API_KEY (or substitute directly into xi-api-key).
primary_path.output: Key available to HTTP clients in the shell
primary_path.success_signal: The environment variable is set for the current shell.
primary_path.failure_or_wait: Shell persistence depends on the OS.

details: Send text and model_id in the JSON body; optionally set output_format=mp3_44100_128 and write the response to a file with --output.
primary_path.input: API key, Free-tier credits, and voice_id
primary_path.success_signal: HTTP 200 with audio bytes for the requested text.
primary_path.failure_or_wait: 401 invalid key; 422 invalid parameters; 429 rate limit or exhausted quota.

primary_path.action: Confirm the response body is playable audio (for example open the saved mp3).
primary_path.success_signal: Audio plays or file size is non-zero for the sample text.
primary_path.failure_or_wait: JSON error body means the first call did not succeed.

branches.condition: The developer prefers the official SDK Quickstart.
branches.path: Install the Python or TypeScript SDK and call text_to_speech.convert for the same voice and text.
branches.effect_on_first_success: Adds package install and optional speaker playback without changing the TTS endpoint boundary.

branches.path: Upgrade subscription or purchase Pay As You Go credits before further API generation.
branches.effect_on_first_success: Billing purchase becomes required after Free quota is gone.

friction_gates.description: Email signup requires email verification before service use.
Free-tier quota is automatic; paid plans or Pay As You Go are required after credits are exhausted.
xi-api-key must authenticate every API request.

sources.title: Billing
evidence_supported: Pay As You Go after exhaustion

uncertainties.question: Exact Free-tier character/credit allotment and commercial-use restrictions for API-generated audio on Free.
uncertainties.impact: May affect how far demos can go before requiring Pay As You Go.
uncertainties.reason_unresolved: Billing points to the pricing/subscription page for plan tables rather than embedding current Free numbers in the billing article.

excluded_after_success: SDK install and speaker playback helpers
Voice cloning and Voice Library workflows
Paid plan upgrades beyond Free first success
